name: toBasicOpen_to_map
nl_statement: For elements r and f in a commutative ring R, restricting the localized element (the image of f in the localization away from s) to the basic open D(s) equals the constant section f/1 on D(s).
nl_assumptions: s, f are elements of a commutative ring R
We have the structure sheaf on Spec R
nl_steps: Apply the localization lift equality property
Use the characterization of the toOpen map as a constant section

name: toBasicOpen_injective
nl_statement: The restriction map toBasicOpen from R to sections of the structure sheaf on D(s) is injective.
nl_assumptions: f is an element of commutative ring R
We work with the localization away from f
The structure sheaf is a sheaf (satisfies descent)
nl_steps: Assume two elements s and t map to the same section
Express both as fractions a/b and c/d using localization
Observe that equality in the sheaf means they agree on D(f)
Define the ideal I of all elements r where r(da - bc) = 0
Show I is indeed an ideal using properties of multiplication
Prove f is in the radical of I (some power of f nullifies the difference)
Use the prime spectrum characterization: f is in radical iff it vanishes nowhere on Z(I)
Conclude that equality in localization implies equality in R by examining prime specializations

name: locally_const_basicOpen
nl_statement: Any section of the structure sheaf on an open set can be represented as a constant fraction f/g on some basic open neighborhood.
nl_assumptions: U is an open set in Spec R
s is a section over U
x is a point in U
Basic opens form a basis of the Zariski topology
nl_steps: By definition of the structure sheaf, s is locally a fraction f/g
Restrict to the basic open containing x (using basis property)
Refine the denominator g by taking a suitable power (nonzero power ensures basicOpen(h^n) = basicOpen(h))
Adjust the numerator f by multiplying by the proportionality constant
Verify compatibility with the restriction map

name: normalize_finite_fraction_representation
nl_statement: Given finitely many fractions a_i/h_i representing a section on a basic open cover, there exists a normalized representation where a_i * h_j = h_i * a_j for all pairs (i,j).
nl_assumptions: U is an open set (specifically basicOpen f)
t is a finite index set
a_i and h_i are element sequences indexed by t
Each pair of fractions a_i/h_i and a_j/h_j agree as sections on the overlap basicOpen(h_i * h_j)
nl_steps: For each pair (i,j), extract from localization equality a power (h_i * h_j)^n where the numerators match
Take the supremum N of all these powers (finitude ensures it exists)
Multiply numerator a_i by h_i^N and denominator h_i by h_i^(N+1) to normalize
The identity basicOpen(h_i^(N+1)) = basicOpen(h_i) ensures the morphisms still work
Verify the normalization condition holds: the adjusted fractions satisfy a'_i * h'_j = h'_i * a'_j
Check that the new fractions still represent the original section by restricting

name: toBasicOpen_surjective
nl_statement: The restriction map toBasicOpen from R to sections of the structure sheaf on D(f) is surjective.
nl_assumptions: f is an element of commutative ring R
basicOpen f is compact
We can cover basicOpen f by finitely many smaller basic opens
The sheaf property allows gluing local sections
nl_steps: Given a section s on basicOpen f, represent it locally as a fraction on each point
Extract a finite subcover from the compactness of basicOpen f
Use the normalization lemma to find a consistent representation: a_i/h_i with a_i * h_j = h_i * a_j
Show that f belongs to the radical of the ideal generated by the h_i (f vanishes nowhere on the basic opens)
Express f^(n+1) as a linear combination of the h_i with coefficients b_i
The preimage of s is the fraction (∑ b_i * a_i) / f^(n+1)
Verify this preimage restricts to s on each basic open using the normalized condition and the sheaf property

name: isIso_toBasicOpen
nl_statement: The map toBasicOpen is an isomorphism of commutative rings (equivalently, a ring equivalence in the categorical sense).
nl_assumptions: toBasicOpen is injective
toBasicOpen is surjective
Ring isomorphisms are detected by the forget functor to sets
nl_steps: Use the injectivity and surjectivity results to establish bijectivity
Apply the criterion that a ring homomorphism is an isomorphism iff its underlying set map is bijective
Use the fact that the forgetful functor from CommRingCat to Set reflects isomorphisms

name: stalkAlgebra
nl_statement: The stalk of the structure sheaf at a prime ideal p has a natural R-algebra structure inherited from the localization map toStalk.
nl_assumptions: p is a prime spectrum point (prime ideal)
The stalk inherits the ring structure and R-action from toStalk
nl_steps: Extract the algebra structure from the underlying ring homomorphism of toStalk

name: IsLocalization.to_stalk
nl_statement: The stalk of the structure sheaf at a prime p is canonically isomorphic to the localization of R at the prime ideal p.asIdeal.
nl_assumptions: p is a prime spectrum point
stalkIso provides a ring isomorphism between the stalk and Localization.AtPrime
Localization.AtPrime is the standard localization at a prime ideal
nl_steps: Use the localization characterization: a ring with a localization-like map satisfies the universal property
Transport the IsLocalization instance via the ring equivalence stalkIso
Verify the algebra maps are compatible after transport

name: openAlgebra
nl_statement: Sections of the structure sheaf over an open set U have a natural R-algebra structure inherited from the localization map toOpen.
nl_assumptions: U is an open set in Spec R
toOpen provides the localization-like map from R to sections over U
nl_steps: Extract the algebra structure from the underlying ring homomorphism of toOpen

name: IsLocalization.to_basicOpen
nl_statement: Sections of the structure sheaf over a basic open D(r) form the localization of R away from r, i.e., R[1/r].
nl_assumptions: r is an element of R
basicOpenIso provides a ring isomorphism between sections on D(r) and Localization.Away r
Localization.Away r is the localization inverting all powers of r
nl_steps: Use the localization characterization via ring equivalence
Transport the IsLocalization instance from Localization.Away via basicOpenIso
Verify the algebra map is correctly identified via localization_toBasicOpen

name: to_basicOpen_epi
nl_statement: The restriction map from R to sections on a basic open D(r) is epic (surjective in the categorical sense).
nl_assumptions: r is an element of R
toOpen R (basicOpen r) is injective with respect to the localization structure
nl_steps: To show a ring homomorphism is epic, show that if two maps agree after composing with it, they are equal
Use the ring extension criterion: equality in a localization is detected by the localization property
Apply the injectivity of the ring homomorphism in the localization

name: to_global_factors
nl_statement: The global sections map toOpen R ⊤ factors through the localization away from 1, which is just R itself.
nl_assumptions: The localization away from 1 is canonically isomorphic to R
basicOpen 1 equals the entire spectrum
nl_steps: Express toOpen R ⊤ as the composition of three maps: R → R[1/1] → sections on basicOpen 1 → sections on ⊤
Observe that R[1/1] = R, so the first factor is an identity
Show the second factor is toBasicOpen R 1
Show the third factor is the restriction map from basicOpen 1 to ⊤ (the whole space)

name: isIso_to_global
nl_statement: The global sections map from R to the structure sheaf's global sections is an isomorphism.
nl_assumptions: The localization away from 1 is the entire ring R (isomorphic)
Localization at 1 is an isomorphism
toBasicOpen R 1 is an isomorphism
nl_steps: Decompose toOpen R ⊤ using to_global_factors
Show the localization away from 1 is an isomorphism (Localization.atOne)
Show toBasicOpen R 1 is an isomorphism (isIso_toBasicOpen)
Compose isomorphisms to conclude toOpen R ⊤ is an isomorphism

name: globalSectionsIso_hom
nl_statement: The homomorphism component of the global sections isomorphism is exactly the map toOpen R ⊤.
nl_assumptions: globalSectionsIso R is the ring equivalence between R and global sections
nl_steps: This is definitional — by construction of globalSectionsIso, its hom component is toOpen R ⊤

name: toStalk_stalkSpecializes
nl_statement: The localization map toStalk is natural with respect to specialization: if x specializes to y, the toStalk maps commute with stalk specialization.
nl_assumptions: x and y are points in Spec R with x specializing to y (x ⤳ y)
stalkSpecializes is the induced map on stalks corresponding to specialization
nl_steps: Unpack the definition of toStalk in terms of toOpen and the germ map
Apply simplification lemmas that show the diagram commutes

name: localizationToStalk_stalkSpecializes
nl_statement: The localization-to-stalk map is natural with respect to specialization: specialization pulls back to localization maps via the localizationMapOfSpecializes.
nl_assumptions: x and y are points in Spec R with x ⤳ y
localizationToStalk is the composition of toStalk with a localization isomorphism
localizationMapOfSpecializes extends localization maps along specialization
nl_steps: Work with ring homomorphisms by using extensionality at the localization level
Apply the localization extendability criterion: equality of ring maps at localizations is detected by equality on the localization
Compose the three localization lifts (for the intermediate structures)
Use the commutativity of toStalk with stalk specialization to conclude
